title: cx task 2026-02-27 03:40
description:
RFC-006 Phase C Review Request

Review the following proposed changes for Phase C. Provide severity-ordered findings (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW). Focus on correctness, edge cases, and feasibility — not style.

## Workstream 1: Hard Cleanup

1a. DELETE `daemon/internal/checkpoint/` (entire package, confirmed zero imports)
1b. REMOVE checkpoint_content deprecation stub from `daemon/cmd/relay/main.go` (lines 811-816):
```go
if env.To == "admin" && env.Kind == "checkpoint_content" {
    log.Printf("deprecated: checkpoint_content message from=%s, ignoring (RFC-006)", env.From)
    _ = logger.Log(logpkg.NewEvent(logpkg.EventTypeCheckpointAck, env.From, "admin").WithMsgID(env.MsgID).WithStatus("ignored:deprecated"))
    continue
}
```
1c. REMOVE EventTypeCheckpointRequest and EventTypeCheckpointAck from `daemon/internal/log/events.go`
1d. REMOVE Fallback B (legacy recovery/checkpoint queries) from `daemon/cmd/context-capture/main.go`
1e. RENAME `checkpoints.log` → `admin-health.log` in admin-health-check.sh and admin-restart-cx.sh
1f. REMOVE `relay checkpoint` subcommand from `bin/relay` (lines 68-136) — it's the only producer of checkpoint_content messages
1g. BULK DELETE ~2018 session-end beads: `bd list --label "source:session_end" | xargs bd delete`

## Workstream 2: Watchdog Liveness in Relay Daemon

Add to `daemon/cmd/relay/main.go`:
- Debounced check in message event loop (action-triggered, no new ticker)
- On each message, if >5min since last check: read `$STATE_DIR/admin-watchdog.heartbeat`, parse unix timestamp
- If stale (>5min) or missing: restart watchdog via `exec.Command("admin-watchdog.sh")` with setsid
- Resolve path via exec.LookPath (deployed to ~/.local/bin/) with fallback
- Propagate RELAY_STATE_DIR, RELAY_LOG_DIR, RELAY_TMUX_SESSION env vars
- ~30 lines of Go

## Workstream 3: CX Auto-Context-Cycle

Upgrade `admin-health-check.sh` CX auto-compact (currently just sends `/compact`) to full cycle:
1. Send `/compact` to CX pane
2. Poll every 5s for `Context compacted` in pane capture (max 30 iterations = 2.5min)
3. On completion, send `/rec` to CX pane
4. Log start/complete/timeout

Watchdog ignores CC/OC context — Claude Code manages its own compaction.

## Workstream 4: Post-Compact Recovery for CC/OC

Two changes:
4a. In `daemon/hooks/pre-compact.sh` (which already fires on CC/OC auto-compact): add `touch "$RELAY_STATE_DIR/needs-rec-${ROLE_SAFE}"` after the checkpoint bead creation. Hook already has RELAY_STATE_DIR and ROLE_SAFE vars. Hook already skips CX (line 8).

4b. In `daemon/scripts/admin/admin-watchdog.sh` main loop (every 30s): check for `needs-rec-{role}` marker files. If found, send `/rec` to the appropriate pane via tmux send-keys, delete marker.

Key: CC/OC compaction is event-driven (Claude auto-compacts → hook fires → marker → watchdog sends /rec). No polling for CC context %.

## Questions for Review
- Any missing cleanup in W1?
- Race conditions in W2 (two restarts if messages burst)?
- W3 polling blocking the health check for up to 2.5min — acceptable?
- W4 marker timing: hook fires before compact completes, watchdog picks up within 30s. Is the /rec too early or too late?

[2026-02-27T04:00:19Z] cx -> oc
RFC-006 Phase C review (cx)\n\nHIGH\n1) Workstream 1f/1b can create hard-fail spam unless 100% atomic rollout with W2/W4: removing 'relay checkpoint' producer + relay checkpoint_content stub is fine, but if any stale producer remains, messages become undeliverable and silently unhandled. Recommend one commit that removes producer + handler + any callers together, with grep gate for checkpoint_content before merge.\n2) Workstream 1g bulk delete risk: 'bd list --label source:session_end | xargs bd delete' is unsafe. If selector is broader than expected or BEADS_DIR/DB target is wrong, deletion is irreversible. Use explicit dry-run count + export/backup + xargs -r + chunked delete with logging.\n3) W2 watchdog restart path is currently fragile unless restart is singleflight/cooldown-guarded. Under burst traffic, stale/missing heartbeat can trigger repeated restarts before heartbeat file updates, causing restart storms. Add restart lock file or in-process CAS flag + min restart interval.\n\nMEDIUM\n1) W1e cleanup is incomplete in current tree: checkpoints.log is also used by daemon/scripts/admin/admin-checkpoint-cycle.sh (not just admin-health-check.sh and admin-restart-cx.sh). Rename all writers/readers together or you split telemetry.\n2) W3 polling in admin-health-check can block script for up to 2.5 min; since admin-loop runs health every 5 min, this consumes ~50% of the cadence and can delay dead-agent restart detection. Feasible, but better moved to async helper/background job or shorter timeout/backoff.\n3) W4 marker timing race: creating needs-rec marker pre-compact means watchdog may send /rec before compact finishes; /rec can be ignored/lost if agent still compacting. Gate /rec send on compact completion signal (pane text match or prompt-ready check), and keep marker until success.\n\nLOW\n1) W1d removing fallback B in context-capture is probably OK, but if any environment still relies on legacy bd show output (without --body), restore-render will degrade to empty checkpoint body.\n2) W1c removing EventTypeCheckpoint* constants is safe if no external consumer depends on those exact event types; update docs/schemas to avoid drift.\n\nAnswers to questions\n- Missing cleanup in W1? Yes: admin-checkpoint-cycle.sh still references checkpoints.log; also update docs/help text mentioning relay checkpoint/checkpoint event types to avoid stale operator guidance.\n- W2 race with burst messages? Yes, unless you add singleflight/cooldown and atomic last-check update before spawn attempt.\n- W3 blocking 2.5min acceptable? Functionally acceptable but operationally weak; recommend async or shorter bounded wait.\n- W4 marker timing too early/late? Too early is the real risk. Use completion/prompt gate before firing /rec; late by <=30s is usually acceptable.

Waiting for OC approval to implement a 3-part infra fix: add cx-checkpoint-inject/tmux-inject to git, fix party-v2's worktree-relative admin-skills path, and delete worktree copies of daemon/admin-skills/.

# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-25 20:59:34 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772071119-f595d200

## Current Goal
Investigating the post-compaction recovery gap: session briefs are created by pre-compact hook but not automatically loaded back after in-session compaction. Researching Claude Code's custom compact instructions feature to inject restore guidance into the compaction summary.

## Key Decisions
- Session brief pipeline verified end-to-end: pre-compact.sh creates brief bead, session-start.sh loads it on next session
- Gap identified: after in-session compaction, the brief exists but is not auto-loaded — had to manually query bd to find it
- post-compact.sh exists in hooks dir but is dead code (never registered in settings.json)
- No PostCompact hook event exists in Claude Code — only PreCompact
- Research found: PreCompact hook stdout gets injected into compaction context, which could be used to inject restore instructions

## Blockers
- Need to determine best mechanism for auto-loading session brief after in-session compaction

## Next Steps
1. Implement solution for post-compaction brief loading (likely via PreCompact stdout injection or custom compact instructions)
2. Clean up dead post-compact.sh file
3. Once recovery flow is solid end-to-end, tag 1.0

title: oc checkpoint 2026-02-25 13:09
description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-25 13:09:49 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772042946-945fe2bd

## Current Goal
Merge CX's paste-buffer rewrite of tmux.go (load-buffer + paste-buffer replacing send-keys -l), rebuild daemon, and test with 20KB payload. CC cross-review complete — race condition concern already addressed in final CX code.

## Key Decisions
- Replaced tmux send-keys -l with load-buffer/paste-buffer for large payload support (send-keys fails at ~20KB)
- Removed all WakePane/resize code (caused pane shrink accumulation on blocked retries)
- Kept per-pane mutex (paneSendLocks) to prevent buffer name race on concurrent sends
- Sleep set to 1s post-paste before Enter — conservative but safe
- Will revisit idle pane wake-up only if issue resurfaces

## Blockers
- (none) — CX code reviewed and ready to merge

## Next Steps
1. Merge cx-wt tmux.go changes to main
2. Rebuild daemon (go build -o ~/.local/bin/relay-daemon ./cmd/relay/)
3. Restart session and test with 20KB RFC-004 payload to CX
4. If working, move to process lifecycle fix (stop using disown)

---

# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-25 12:38 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772041128-f3ba2886

## Current Goal
Testing injection with small vs large payloads. Discovered tmux send-keys fails with exit status 1 on 20KB payload — chunking needed for large messages after all.

## Key Decisions
- Pre-send wake-up works (SIGWINCH before payload)
- Prompt gating works (detects CX prompt correctly)
- tmux send-keys -l fails on large payloads (~20KB) with exit status 1 — this is the actual failure mode
- Need chunking in SendToPane for large payloads
- Testing smaller payload now to confirm wake-up + gating work when size isn't the issue

## Blockers
- Large payload injection fails without chunking

## Next Steps
1. Confirm small payload delivery works
2. Implement chunking in SendToPane (split at ~4KB with inter-chunk delay)
3. Retest with full 20KB RFC
4. RFC-004 review still pending

title: oc checkpoint 2026-02-26 00:52
description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-26 01:11 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772085110-c094aafa

## Current Goal
Investigating and fixing critical bugs in bin/party-stop. The script self-kills when run from inside the tmux session it's tearing down, which also broke the recovery pipeline (async brief workers died, leaving empty checkpoint beads for session recovery).

## Key Decisions
- Confirmed self-kill is the primary production bug (CX corroborated). When `party stop` is invoked from inside tmux, line 195 kills the session including the calling process.
- Kill order is actually correct (daemons first, tmux last) — my initial analysis was wrong, CX corrected.
- Two-phase fix approach agreed: Phase 1 hotfix (self-detect + re-exec detached via setsid with PARTY_STOP_DETACHED=1 guard), Phase 2 hardening (restore pre-kill checkpoint dispatch, worker verification, project-context session resolution).
- The 5-pass review never covered party-stop itself — only adjacent files were in the diff. The async rewrite (97db6cc) was the last CC commit and went unreviewed.
- User raised UX concern: how does party-stop find the right session when run from outside tmux with multiple sessions? Currently requires explicit arg or lists sessions. Project-context resolution deferred to phase 2.

## Blockers
- Waiting for user decision on whether to proceed with phase 1 hotfix now

## Next Steps
1. Get user go-ahead, then have CC implement phase 1 self-detach hotfix
2. Phase 2: checkpoint dispatch restoration + async worker reliability
3. Ensure proper 5-pass review covers party-stop this time

---

5-pass code review of ALL Phase A relay daemon changes. Review the full task bead manager code in daemon/cmd/relay/main.go on main branch (~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go).

5 passes:
1. Correctness — logic errors, off-by-one, race conditions, nil derefs
2. Security — injection, untrusted input, command construction 
3. Reliability — error handling, timeouts, resource leaks, goroutine leaks
4. Performance — blocking calls, unnecessary allocations, hot path issues
5. Maintainability — naming, structure, dead code, confusing logic

Report findings per pass, severity-ordered. This is the final review before Phase B.

[2026-02-26T18:20:26Z] cx -> oc
5-pass review of main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go

Pass 1 — Correctness
HIGH-1: Stale classifier can still overwrite bead status after msg_seq advanced. In runClassifier, state is checked (lines 382-385) before bd update (386). If a newer message increments msg_seq between check and update, old classifier result can still apply to bead status; touchStateIfMatch only protects local state file afterward. This violates RFC stale-result discard semantics.
Ref: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:382-390

HIGH-2: OC->agent path can clobber newer bead_status in state file. handleOCToAgent loads state, appends note, then saves old struct with incremented msg_seq (lines 410-458). If classifier/sweeper updated status concurrently, this save can revert bead_status (e.g., completed->open), causing wrong reuse decisions.
Ref: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:410-458

LOW-1: createTaskBead omits explicit --status open even though RFC requires explicit open on create.
Ref: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:257-265

Pass 2 — Security
No HIGH/MEDIUM findings.
LOW-1: Unbounded payload is passed as CLI arg (--description/--append-notes). Not shell-injection (exec.Command is safe), but very large payloads can trigger argument-size failures/DoS-like behavior.
Refs: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:220-221,254-266

Pass 3 — Reliability
HIGH-1: Message delivery is blocked on synchronous bead operations. Event loop runs handleAgentToOC/handleOCToAgent before injector routing; these do blocking bd calls (append/show/create/update). If bd stalls, relay delivery stalls, violating RFC async/non-blocking expectation for agent->OC responses.
Refs: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:769-779,839-842,410-459,461-505

MEDIUM-1: Stale sweep and classifier both mutate bead status without a coordination strategy beyond best-effort state checks; status thrash is possible under timing races (e.g., stale set right as classifier completes).
Refs: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:321-343,376-390

Pass 4 — Performance
HIGH-1: Hot path performs multiple external process launches per message (bd update + optional bd show + codex). This is in the watcher event loop and directly increases end-to-end routing latency under load.
Refs: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:769-779,220-227,345-355,461-505

MEDIUM-1: resolveBDPath/LookPath+stat runs on every bd call via buildBDCommand; avoidable repeated filesystem lookups in high-traffic path.
Refs: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:173-204,206-217

Pass 5 — Maintainability
MEDIUM-1: Concurrency model is split across two mutexes (mu/stateMu) plus external side effects, making happens-before guarantees hard to reason about; race-prone logic is spread across runClassifier/handle* paths.
Refs: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:83-90,302-390,410-505

Ref: ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/main/daemon/cmd/relay/main.go:83-87

title: cx task 2026-02-27 05:50
description:
RFC-006 Phase C - Updated Design Review

Two changes since your last review. Provide severity-ordered findings.

## Change 1: CX Injection Fix (already merged)

The Codex suggestion prompt (› Run /review...) was blocking message delivery. Fixed in 3 commits:
- IsPaneReady detects suggestion via codexFooterVisible() — checks for "% left ·" or "% context left" in captured pane text
- When suggestion detected in run() loop: send space (dismisses suggestion), 200ms sleep, send backspace (clears space), 200ms sleep, then inject immediately instead of requeueing
- This is already deployed and tested working

## Change 2: Relay Pane-Status CLI Endpoint (new for Phase C)

Problem: Both the injector (Go) and the watchdog/health-check (bash) parse CX pane state independently with hardcoded patterns. When Codex UI changes (like the suggestion prompt), multiple places break.

Solution: Relay daemon owns ALL pane parsing. Expose a CLI endpoint:
```
relay-daemon --pane-status cx
# Returns: {"ready": true, "context_pct": 84, "suggestion_active": false, "compacted": false, "idle": true}
```

The watchdog calls this instead of doing its own tmux capture + grep. Benefits:
- One place to update when Codex changes prompt format
- IsPaneReady, codexFooterVisible, context % extraction all live in Go in the relay
- Watchdog gets clean JSON, no pattern matching
- Live read on demand (not cached/stale) — relay does a fresh tmux capture when asked

This changes W2-W4:
- W2: Add --pane-status CLI to relay daemon binary
- W3: Health check calls relay-daemon --pane-status cx for context % and idle state
- W4: Watchdog calls relay-daemon --pane-status cc/oc to detect post-compact state

The health check's current CX-specific pane parsing (lines 96-113 in admin-health-check.sh) gets replaced with a call to the endpoint.

## Questions
- Is a CLI flag the right interface, or should it be a unix socket?
- Should the endpoint return all panes at once or one at a time?
- Any concerns about the relay binary being used for both daemon and CLI modes?
notes:
[2026-02-27T05:51:16Z] cx -> oc
RFC-006 Phase C updated design review (cx)

HIGH
1) Feasibility blocker: if relay-daemon pane-status mode reuses current startup flow, it will hit singleton lock acquisition and fail whenever daemon is already running. Pane-status mode must short-circuit before lock and watcher startup, or use a separate subcommand path.

MEDIUM
1) Single source of truth can still drift unless parsing logic is factored into one shared Go function used by both injector readiness checks and pane-status output.
2) Health-check resilience: if scripts depend only on pane-status and the CLI returns non-zero, health automation can degrade silently. Define explicit error contract and fallback behavior.
3) W3/W4 semantics: boolean compacted from tail text is fragile without recency metadata. Include compacted_at or recent_compact_seconds to avoid stale decisions.
4) Polling overhead: one process per check is acceptable at current cadence, but one-pane-at-a-time causes extra tmux captures and inconsistent snapshots.

LOW
1) Add schema_version to pane-status JSON for compatibility guards in scripts.
2) Consider exposing raw_tail_hash for debug and observability without re-parsing in bash.

Answers
- CLI vs unix socket: CLI is the right first interface for Phase C, provided pane-status is a standalone code path that does not require daemon lock acquisition. Socket can be added later if polling volume justifies it.
- All panes or one pane: Prefer both. Default to all panes for coherent snapshot and fewer tmux calls, with optional role filter for script ergonomics.
- One binary for daemon and CLI: acceptable with guardrails. Parse args first, branch early into CLI mode, avoid daemon side effects in CLI mode, and keep parser logic in one internal package.

[2026-02-27T05:55:23Z] oc -> cx
Phase C Task: W2 — Pane-Status CLI Endpoint

## Goal
Add a --pane-status subcommand to relay-daemon that returns parsed pane state as JSON. This becomes the single source of truth for pane state — both the injector and the watchdog/health-check consume it.

## Design

### Early branching in main()
Parse os.Args BEFORE any daemon startup (lock, watcher, etc). If --pane-status is present, branch into CLI mode and exit. No daemon side effects.

```go
func main() {
    // CLI subcommands — must come before daemon startup
    if len(os.Args) > 1 && os.Args[1] == "--pane-status" {
        runPaneStatus(os.Args[2:])
        return
    }
    // ... existing daemon startup code ...
}
```

### runPaneStatus behavior
- Accepts optional role filter: `relay-daemon --pane-status` (all) or `relay-daemon --pane-status cx` (one)
- Reads pane map from $RELAY_STATE_DIR/panes.json (same as daemon)
- For each pane, does a live tmux capture-pane
- Parses state using shared functions
- Outputs JSON to stdout

### Output format
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "panes": {
    "cx": {
      "ready": false,
      "context_pct": 84,
      "suggestion_active": true,
      "compacted": false,
      "compacted_ago_s": -1,
      "idle": true
    },
    "cc": {
      "ready": true,
      "context_pct": -1,
      "suggestion_active": false,
      "compacted": false,
      "compacted_ago_s": -1,
      "idle": true
    }
  }
}
```

- context_pct: -1 if not detectable (CC doesn't show it in pane)
- compacted_ago_s: seconds since compact detected in pane text, -1 if not detected
- compacted: true if compact completion marker visible in recent pane text

### Shared parsing package
Create `daemon/internal/pane/` package with:
- `ParsePaneState(target, capturedText string) PaneState` — the core parser
- Move `codexFooterVisible` logic here
- Move prompt prefix detection here
- Move context % extraction here (currently only in bash)
- CX compact marker: "Context compacted"
- CC compact marker: "✻ Conversation compacted"

### Injector integration
Update `daemon/internal/tmux/injector.go`:
- `IsPaneReady` calls into the shared `pane.ParsePaneState` instead of its own prefix matching
- `codexFooterVisible` moves to the pane package
- The suggestion dismissal logic in `run()` stays in the injector (it's injection-specific behavior)

### Detection patterns

**CX (Codex):**
- Ready: last non-empty line starts with `›` AND no footer visible
- Suggestion active: `›` present AND footer line contains `% left ·` or `% context left`
- Context %: `\d+(?=% context left)` from footer
- Idle: footer visible (? for shortcuts or % context left)
- Compacted: "Context compacted" in captured text

**CC/OC (Claude Code):**
- Ready: last non-empty line starts with `❯`
- Context %: not detectable from pane capture (-1)
- Idle: `❯` visible at prompt
- Compacted: "✻ Conversation compacted" in captured text

### Files to create/modify
- CREATE: `daemon/internal/pane/pane.go` — shared parser
- CREATE: `daemon/internal/pane/pane_test.go` — unit tests with sample pane captures
- EDIT: `daemon/cmd/relay/main.go` — add early --pane-status branching + runPaneStatus()
- EDIT: `daemon/internal/tmux/injector.go` — use pane.ParsePaneState in IsPaneReady

### Build verification
```
cd daemon && go build ./... && go test ./... && go vet ./...
```

### Important
- CX can't commit — OC will commit for you. Just relay me when done with the code.
- The pane map is at $RELAY_STATE_DIR/panes.json — resolve RELAY_STATE_DIR from env or default to ~/.relay/state
- Exit 0 on success, exit 1 on error (with stderr message)
- schema_version: 1 in output

[2026-02-27T05:57:38Z] cx -> oc
W2 implementation complete in cx-wt.

Changed files:
- daemon/internal/pane/pane.go (new shared parser)
- daemon/internal/pane/pane_test.go (new unit tests)
- daemon/cmd/relay/main.go (early --pane-status branch + runPaneStatus)
- daemon/internal/tmux/injector.go (IsPaneReady now uses pane.ParsePaneState)

What was implemented:
- Early CLI branch in main() before daemon startup side effects (lock/watcher/etc).
- `relay-daemon --pane-status [oc|cc|cx]`:
  - Reads pane map from `$RELAY_PANE_MAP` or `$RELAY_STATE_DIR/panes.json`
  - Defaults state dir to `~/.relay/state` when RELAY_STATE_DIR is unset
  - Live `tmux capture-pane` per requested pane(s)
  - Outputs JSON with `schema_version: 1` and `panes` map

  - CX footer/suggestion detection (`% left ·` / `% context left`)
  - prompt readiness by role
  - context percent extraction
  - compact markers (CX: `Context compacted`, CC/OC: `✻ Conversation compacted`)
  - `compacted_ago_s` extraction when duration text is present, else 0 when marker is visible
- Injector readiness uses shared parser instead of duplicated prefix logic.

Validation run:
- `cd daemon && go build ./...` ✅
- `cd daemon && go test ./...` ✅
- `cd daemon && go vet ./...` ✅

Notes:
- I also manually exercised `go run ./cmd/relay --pane-status cx`; it correctly exits 1 with stderr when the pane id in pane-map is invalid/stale.
- No commit was made (per instruction, OC will commit).

## Current Goal
Investigating pane ID staleness regression. User frustrated by recurring stale panes.json issue. Completed thorough research — confirmed this is a regression from Feb 22 admin-loop refactor that deleted the auto-detection logic.

## Key Decisions
- Session brief pipeline verified working end-to-end (pre-compact hook creates bead, session-start loads it)
- Gap identified: post-compaction brief not auto-loaded within same session (no PostCompact hook exists in Claude Code)
- Pane staleness is a REGRESSION: IsPaneMapStale() and refreshPaneMapIfStale() were deleted in commit 867e7c9 when adminpane package was removed
- admin-register-panes.sh exists but nothing calls it automatically
- Hot-reload (10s poll) only fires when file changes — no component detects that pane IDs themselves are invalid

## Blockers
- User wants thorough investigation before any fix attempts — do not attempt to fix pane staleness yet

## Next Steps
1. Present pane staleness findings to user (DONE — awaiting user response)
2. User will decide approach for permanent fix (likely: health-check validates pane IDs against tmux, or admin-loop calls register-panes on startup)
3. Then address post-compaction brief loading gap
4. Tag 1.0 after both issues resolved

description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-19 01:26 ET
**Role:** cc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1771482354-2b591df3

## Current Goal
Awaiting OC direction on RFC-003 implementation. Read RFC-003 and implementation plan; ready to implement pre-compact hook v2 (Chunks 1.1-1.6) if assigned to CC, or to support CX-assigned work.

## Key Decisions
- RFC-003 session briefs supersede RFC-002 summary watcher — one-shot background LLM call from pre-compact hook at compaction boundaries
- Pre-filtering JSONL to plain text before LLM call is essential (31x compression, 400KB → 12KB, saves ~40s)
- Background job must use nohup/disown pattern to survive hook exit
- Offset tracking: two-phase commit — advance only after worker script launched, not after LLM call
- Brief anchored to checkpoint bead ID (not chk_id token) via label `checkpoint:{bead_id}`

## Blockers
- OC hasn't confirmed role assignment (CX vs CC for Chunks 1.1-1.6)
- OC needs to write `party-brief-prompt.txt` (Chunk 1.4) before LLM call can be wired

## Next Steps
1. Await OC's response on task assignment routing
2. If assigned: read current `~/.claude/hooks/pre-compact.sh` before modifying
3. Implement Chunks 1.1-1.3 (stdin parse, offset tracking, filter script) in sequence
4. Wire LLM call (1.4) once OC delivers prompt template
5. Complete 1.5-1.6 (bead write + hygiene)

Fixing CX checkpoint integration. Codex ignores prompt arguments and doesn't follow bash steps literally. Tested shell expansion, natural language, and file handoff — all failed. Considering having admin write CX beads directly from pane tail capture instead.

- Created tmux-inject wrapper for reliable pane injection (text, 500ms, Enter)
- All admin skills switched from relay to direct tmux injection for slash commands

description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-19 10:51 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1771516271-98651ca3

## Current Goal
Applying 8 critical fixes to pre-compact.sh and restore.md (from /5pass review). CC done with CRIT-2/4/5/6/7. CX re-tasked after compact to design CRIT-1 (success marker) and CRIT-3 (flock). Waiting on CX specs for CC to apply final two.

## Key Decisions
- CC applies all code changes (CX sandbox can't write ~/.claude/hooks/)
- CX designs CRIT-1 success marker protocol: worker writes marker after bd create, parent polls before offset commit
- CX designs CRIT-3 flock: role-specific lock file, skip-if-held (not wait), covers LAST_OFFSET read through commit_compact_offset
- CRIT-8 (magic numbers config) deferred
- Phase 5 (compact-on-idle) added to RFC-003 implementation plan as TODO
- /compact confirmed working on CX via tmux injection (tested this session)

## Blockers
- Waiting on CX CRIT-1/3 specs (CX just compacted, re-tasked)

## Next Steps
1. Receive CX specs for CRIT-1 + CRIT-3
2. CC applies both to pre-compact.sh
3. CX cross-reviews full pre-compact.sh
4. Commit all Phase 1 + critical fixes to oc-wt branch

description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-25 10:42 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772034178-6a36e457

## Current Goal
Synthesized CC and CX proposals for daemon reliability fixes (3 structural bugs + watchdog). Waiting for user to approve priorities before dispatching implementation.

## Key Decisions
- Investigated cc-party daemon silent death: inconclusive root cause, 3 structural bugs identified
- CC proposed pragmatic fixes (pgrep sweep, circuit breaker, startup banner, kill-0 watchdog)
- CX proposed deeper fixes (instance IDs, tombstone files, heartbeat, reconciliation ticker, systemd units)
- Synthesized priority: P0=watcher resilience + crash forensics, P1=orphan cleanup + watchdog, P2=heartbeat/systemd

## Blockers
- Waiting for user approval on priority ordering before dispatching implementation

## Next Steps
1. Get user approval on fix priorities
2. Dispatch P0 fixes to CC (watcher.go) and CX (main.go forensics)
3. Dispatch P1 fixes (bin/party orphan cleanup, admin-loop watchdog)
4. Rebuild daemon, restart both party sessions
5. Send RFC-004 to CX for critique (deferred from earlier)

---

Fix party multi-agent checkpoint pipeline end-to-end. Specifically: verify `bd create` works after PATH fix in relay daemon.

- Fixed 5 bugs in commit 76d465e: bd PATH in systemd service, checkpoint-cycle.md injection wrappers, live admin skills sync, systemd env file name, stale comment
- Relay daemon rebuilt from clean main and restarted with explicit PATH
- `bd create` still failing — stale event from old daemon cycle may be cause; investigating

## Blockers

- Unclear if error is from old daemon run or new one (cycle key is time-bucketed, may be replaying)

3. If still failing, check relay daemon source for where PATH is used when exec'ing bd

description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-26 02:12 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772088775-8047a811

## Current Goal
Fixing party-stop bugs. Found critical cross-session RELAY_STATE_DIR contamination: when run from inside a different party session, party-stop uses the caller's state dir instead of the target's. Kills target tmux but cleans wrong state. CC implementing fix + diagnostics. CX will review.

## Key Decisions
- CX identified root cause: STATE_DIR=${RELAY_STATE_DIR:-} on line 48 picks up caller env before querying target tmux env. Cross-session invocation cleans wrong state dir.
- Fix approach: query target session's tmux env FIRST, fall back to caller env only if target unavailable, warn on fallback.
- CC has 2 diagnostics commits (6a3941f, 63a15f9) ready + working on state dir fix. All will be CX-reviewed before merge.
- Manually killed orphaned cc-party relay-daemon (pid 3093904) that party-stop left alive.
- My earlier theory about setsid redirect was wrong — CX confirmed redirects work fine with setsid. Missing log means detach branch didn't fire (ran from different session so CURRENT_SESSION != SESSION).

## Blockers
- Waiting for CC to complete state dir resolution fix + CX review

## Next Steps
1. Wait for CC commit + CX review of state dir fix
2. Get direct CX confirmation
3. Review diff, merge to main
4. Run install.sh, retest party-stop cross-session

description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-19 15:11 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1771530665-6b5e3cd0

## Current Goal
Continue RFC-003 critical fixes: re-task CX on CRIT-1 (success marker two-phase commit) and CRIT-3 (flock for concurrent hooks) design specs, then have CC apply them. All other criticals (CRIT-2/4/5/6/7) already done by CC.

## Key Decisions
- CRIT-1/3 require CX design specs before CC can implement — CX repeatedly interrupted (compact test, bad checkpoint injection)
- cx-checkpoint-inject wrapper script created to prevent admin model from adding extra args to CX checkpoint injection
- pre-compact hook session_brief bead write failed on this compaction (worker ran, bd create failed) — noted for CRIT-1 context
- Phase 5 (compact-on-idle) added to RFC-003 as todo, not starting until criticals done

## Blockers
- CX needs to deliver CRIT-1 and CRIT-3 design specs — currently unblocked (new session after compact)

## Next Steps
1. Re-task CX with CRIT-1 and CRIT-3 design specs (designs from recovery file §5)
2. CC applies CRIT-1 + CRIT-3 when specs arrive
3. CX cross-reviews full pre-compact.sh after all 7 criticals applied
4. Commit all changes to oc-wt branch
5. Investigate session_brief bead write failure (worker log: "bead write failed")

title: cc task 2026-02-27 04:05
description:
Priority fix: CX message injection is broken after Codex update.

## Problem
Codex now shows suggestion prompts like:
```
› Run /review on my current changes

  gpt-5.3-codex default · 84% left · ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/cx-wt
```

The injector's `IsPaneReady` sees `›` and thinks CX is ready for input. But it's a suggestion, not the input prompt. Messages get pasted on top of the suggestion and garbled.

## Signal
When CX has a suggestion showing (NOT ready), the Codex footer line is visible below:
```
  gpt-5.3-codex default · 84% left · ~/Sandbox/personal/new_party/cx-wt
```
When the user has actually typed input (ready), that footer disappears.

## Fix
In `daemon/internal/tmux/injector.go`, modify `IsPaneReady` (line 268). After the existing `›` prefix match succeeds, add a CX-specific check: if the target is `cx` and the captured pane text contains a Codex footer pattern (`% left ·` or `% context left`), return NOT ready.

Something like:
```go
// After line 292 where › matches:
if target == "cx" && strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(last), "›") {
    if codexFooterVisible(out) {
        return false, strings.TrimSpace(out), nil
    }
}
```

Add a helper:
```go
func codexFooterVisible(paneText string) bool {
    for _, line := range strings.Split(paneText, "\n") {
        trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
        if strings.Contains(trimmed, "% left ·") || strings.Contains(trimmed, "% context left") {
            return true
        }
    }
    return false
}
```

## Files
- `daemon/internal/tmux/injector.go` — IsPaneReady function (line 268-297)

## Important
- The `›` check must come BEFORE the generic prefix loop at line 291, or be inserted as a special case within it
- Only applies when target is "cx" — CC and OC use `❯` not `›`
- Build verify: `cd daemon && go build ./...`
- Commit when done and relay me the result
notes:
[2026-02-27T04:05:54Z] cc -> oc
Fix committed: 8b30a1c — skip CX injection when Codex suggestion prompt is active. In IsPaneReady, when › prefix matches for target cx, we now check for the Codex footer line (% left ·) in captured pane text. If visible → suggestion is active → return not-ready. Build + vet clean. Ready for merge.

[2026-02-27T04:06:42Z] oc -> cc
Follow-up: The fix correctly detects the suggestion prompt (blocks injection — good). But now CX messages are permanently blocked because CX sits at the suggestion prompt forever.

We need one more step: when IsPaneReady detects CX suggestion mode, send Escape to dismiss the suggestion, then on the next retry the prompt will be clean and injection succeeds.

In the run() loop in injector.go, after IsPaneReady returns false for the CX suggestion case, we need to send Escape to the pane. Or better: in IsPaneReady itself, when we detect the Codex suggestion footer, send Escape before returning false. That way the next retry (after backoff) will find a clean prompt.

Add to the codex footer check in IsPaneReady:
```go
if prefix == "›" && target == "cx" && codexFooterVisible(out) {
    // Dismiss the suggestion so next retry finds clean prompt
    i.tmux.Run("send-keys", "-t", paneID, "Escape")
    return false, strings.TrimSpace(out), nil
}
```

Build, vet, commit, relay me result.

[2026-02-27T04:07:02Z] cc -> oc
Follow-up committed: 7f48891 — send Escape to dismiss Codex suggestion before retry. When IsPaneReady detects the footer, it now sends Escape to the pane so the next backoff retry finds a clean prompt. Build + vet clean. Ready for merge.

[2026-02-27T04:08:21Z] oc -> cc
Escape doesn't work for dismissing Codex suggestions — it cycles through modal states (edit previous, transcript view). The correct approach is: type a space then backspace to dismiss the suggestion.

Update the codex footer fix in `daemon/internal/tmux/injector.go`. Replace:
```go
_, _ = i.tmux.Run("send-keys", "-t", paneID, "Escape")
```
with:
```go
// Type space to dismiss suggestion, then backspace to clear it
_, _ = i.tmux.Run("send-keys", "-t", paneID, " ")
time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
_, _ = i.tmux.Run("send-keys", "-t", paneID, "BSpace")
```

You'll need to add "time" to imports if not already there. Build, vet, commit, relay me result.

[2026-02-27T04:08:48Z] cc -> oc
Fixed: 860bdc0 — replaced Escape with space+backspace (100ms gap) to dismiss Codex suggestion. Build + vet clean. Ready for merge.

[2026-02-27T04:09:50Z] oc -> cc
The space+backspace approach has a timing issue: by the time IsPaneReady returns false, backs off, and retries, the suggestion has reappeared. We need to dismiss and inject atomically.

New approach: don't try to dismiss from IsPaneReady. Instead, change the logic in the run() loop. When IsPaneReady returns false AND the error text indicates CX suggestion (codex footer visible), dismiss the suggestion and immediately proceed to inject instead of requeueing.

In injector.go `run()` method, around line 218-230:

Replace the current blocked logic:
```go
ready, tail, err := injector.IsPaneReady(paneID, pq.target)
if err != nil || !ready {
    ...blocked/requeue logic...
    continue
}
```

With:
```go
ready, tail, err := injector.IsPaneReady(paneID, pq.target)
if err != nil || !ready {
    // CX suggestion detected — dismiss and inject immediately
    if pq.target == "cx" && codexFooterVisible(tail) {
        _, _ = injector.tmux.Run("send-keys", "-t", paneID, " ")
        time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)
        _, _ = injector.tmux.Run("send-keys", "-t", paneID, "BSpace")
        time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)
        // Fall through to inject below instead of requeueing
    } else {
        if tail == "" && err != nil {
            tail = err.Error()
        }
        injector.logEvent(logpkg.EventTypeBlocked, item.env.From, pq.target, item.env.MsgID, truncateForLog(tail))
        item.backoff = nextBackoff(item.backoff)
        pq.requeueFront(item)
        if !sleepOrDone(ctx, item.backoff) {
            return
        }
        continue
    }
}
```

Also revert the IsPaneReady changes — remove the space+backspace and the codexFooterVisible check from there. IsPaneReady should just detect and report; the run() loop handles the action.

Build, vet, commit, relay result.

[2026-02-27T04:10:23Z] cc -> oc
Fixed: 3717b53 — moved suggestion dismissal from IsPaneReady into run() loop. IsPaneReady now just detects (returns false + tail with footer). run() checks codexFooterVisible(tail), sends space+backspace with 200ms gaps, then falls through to inject immediately instead of requeueing. Build + vet clean. Ready for merge.

Waiting for OC approval to implement a 3-part infra fix: add cx-checkpoint-inject/tmux-inject to git, fix party-v2's worktree-relative admin-skills path, and delete worktree copies of daemon/admin-skills/.

## Key Decisions
- Recommended canonical path `~/.party/admin-skills/` (consistent with existing `~/.party/` convention in party-v2)
- Part 2 must come AFTER Part 3 is deployed (party-v2 exits with error if dir missing)

5. Delete daemon/admin-skills/ from all 3 worktrees

# Checkpoint

**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772001755-2cb05292

## Current Goal
Completed investigation of cc-party relay daemon silent death. Identified 3 structural bugs. User explicitly said no fixes yet.

## Key Decisions
- cc-party daemon (PID 2347700) died silently at 23:21:39 Feb 24 — exact cause inconclusive
- Orphaned admin-loop PID 2122892 (from 16:20 earlier launch) still running in cc-party context
- Three bugs identified: (1) orphan process leak in bin/party, (2) fatal fsnotify handling in watcher.go, (3) no crash forensics/logging
- Bug #2 (fatal fsnotify) is most plausible daemon death mechanism

## Blockers
- User wants investigation only, no implementation yet
- Core dumps disabled (ulimit -c 0), no journal entries — forensic trail is cold

## Next Steps
1. Wait for user direction on which bugs to fix and priority
2. Fix bin/party orphan cleanup, watcher.go resilience, daemon logging (when approved)
3. Restart cc-party with fixes
4. Send RFC-004 to CX for critique
5. Remove summarywatcher dead code

title: cc task 2026-02-26 18:19
description:
5-pass code review of ALL Phase A context-capture changes. Review the fallback chain code in daemon/cmd/context-capture/main.go on main branch.

5 passes:
1. Correctness — logic errors, wrong queries, missing fallbacks
2. Security — command injection, untrusted input
3. Reliability — error handling, timeouts, graceful degradation
4. Performance — unnecessary subprocess spawns, blocking calls
5. Maintainability — naming, structure, dead code

Report findings per pass, severity-ordered.
notes:
[2026-02-26T18:21:14Z] cc -> oc
5-pass code review of Phase A context-capture changes (daemon/cmd/context-capture/main.go on main, commits 21e3f2d + 2da2fa6).

## Pass 1: Correctness

**HIGH-1: Fallback A uses --closed-after but bd status is 'completed' not 'closed'**
Line 224: `queryBeadWithExtra(bdPath, "task", role, "completed", "--closed-after", twoHoursAgo)`. The --closed-after flag filters by close date, which is a bd concept tied to the 'closed' status lifecycle. When relay writes beads with --status completed (per RFC-006), bd may not populate the closed_at timestamp that --closed-after filters on. Verified bd accepts arbitrary status strings, but the --closed-after filter may never match completed beads if bd only sets closed_at for status=closed transitions.
Fix: Use --created-after as the time filter (task beads are short-lived, created_at is a reasonable proxy for recency), or verify that bd populates closed_at for any terminal status transition.

**MEDIUM-1: Labels parsed as map but bd returns array**
fetchRecentChunkSummaries (line 361) casts `bead["labels"].(map[string]any)` but bd returns labels as a JSON array of strings like ["role:cc", "end_offset:12345"], not a map. The end_offset extraction will never match, so lastSummaryOffset is always 0, and the overlap-skip optimization is silently disabled. (Pre-existing bug, not introduced by Phase A, but affects the recovery pipeline.)
Fix: Parse labels as []any, iterate, and split on ':'.

**LOW-1: queryActiveTaskBead uses created_at for recency but updated_at is more accurate**
A bead created hours ago but recently transitioned to blocked (via classifier) would have an old created_at. If a newer bead was created and already completed/stale, the old blocked bead wins on created_at but the user's intent is the most recently active task.
Fix: Prefer updated_at if available, fall back to created_at.

## Pass 2: Security

**No findings.** All bd invocations use exec.Command (no shell), so role/label values can't inject arguments. AGENT_ROLE is infrastructure-controlled. Bead IDs from bd output are passed as single args to exec.Command — safe.

## Pass 3: Reliability

**HIGH-2: No subprocess timeouts**
All exec.Command calls (bd list, bd show) run without context timeout. If bd hangs (e.g., locked database, network mount stall), restore-render blocks indefinitely. This is the RECOVERY path — it must be robust against infrastructure failures.
Fix: Use exec.CommandContext with a reasonable timeout (e.g., 10s per call). The fallback chain already handles errors gracefully, so a timeout just triggers the next fallback.

**LOW-2: fetchBeadBody falls back from --body to bare show**
If `bd show <id> --body` returns empty, it retries with `bd show <id>` (full output). The full output includes metadata (status, labels, etc.) mixed with content. This fallback may inject non-content metadata into the recovery context, confusing the agent.
Fix: Accept empty body as empty rather than falling back to full show, or parse the full show output to extract only the description field.

## Pass 4: Performance

**MEDIUM-2: Up to 12 subprocess spawns in worst-case fallback chain**
queryActiveTaskBead: 3 bd list + 1 bd show = 4. Fallback A: 1 list + 1 show = 2. Fallback B: 2 list + 1 show = 3. Fallback C: 1 list + 1 show = 2. Session brief supplement: 1 list + 1 show = 2. Total worst case: ~13 subprocesses. Each bd invocation has startup overhead.
Fix: For queryActiveTaskBead, consider a single bd list query without --status filter, then filter in Go. Or add bd support for --status-any (comma-separated).

**LOW-3: bdPath resolved independently in 4 functions**
fetchCheckpoint, fetchSessionBrief, fetchLatestStateRollup, fetchRecentChunkSummaries all call os.ExpandEnv + exec.LookPath independently. Minor overhead, but also a maintainability issue.
Fix: Resolve once in runRestoreRender and pass as parameter.

## Pass 5: Maintainability

**MEDIUM-3: queryBead is a subset of queryBeadWithExtra**
queryBead could delegate to queryBeadWithExtra with no extra args, eliminating code duplication.

**LOW-4: 'age unknown' hardcoded in render output**
Line 165: `fmt.Printf("**Checkpoint:** %s (%s, %s)", checkpointID, checkpointSource, "age unknown")`. Should compute actual age from bead metadata (created_at or updated_at from the JSON response).

**LOW-5: checkpoint-template subcommand is now dead code path**
RFC-006 Phase B removes checkpoint skill/prompt. The checkpoint-template subcommand will become dead code after Phase B. Mark with a TODO or remove proactively.

## Summary
- 2 HIGH (--closed-after filter mismatch, no subprocess timeouts)
- 3 MEDIUM (labels array vs map, subprocess count, code duplication)
- 4 LOW (created_at vs updated_at, bare show fallback, bdPath duplication, dead code)

The fallback chain structure is correct and well-ordered per RFC-006. Error handling degrades gracefully. The HIGHs are the priority: the timeout issue is critical for a recovery path, and the --closed-after filter needs verification against bd's actual behavior with custom statuses.

[2026-02-26T18:21:39Z] oc -> cc
5-pass review fixes for context-capture (your findings). Address these in daemon/cmd/context-capture/main.go on cc-wt:

HIGH-1: Replace --closed-after with --created-after for fallback A (recently completed tasks). bd may not populate closed_at for custom status transitions.

HIGH-2: Add exec.CommandContext with 10s timeout to ALL bd subprocess calls. The recovery path must not hang on a stalled bd process.

MEDIUM-1: Fix labels parsing — bd returns labels as JSON array of strings, not a map. Parse as []any, split on ':'. (Pre-existing bug but fix it now.)

MEDIUM-2: Reduce subprocess count — queryActiveTaskBead should use a single bd list without --status filter, then filter in Go.

MEDIUM-3: queryBead should delegate to queryBeadWithExtra.

LOW-3: Resolve bdPath once and pass as parameter.

Commit on cc-wt when done.

[2026-02-26T18:24:17Z] cc -> oc
All 6 review findings fixed in commit 46a3e19 on cc-wt. Summary:

- HIGH-1: --closed-after → --created-after for fallback A
- HIGH-2: All bd calls use bdRun() with 10s context timeout (go vet clean, no context leaks)
- MEDIUM-1: Labels parsed as []any with prefix match instead of map cast
- MEDIUM-2: queryActiveTaskBead does single bd list (limit 10), filters active statuses in Go
- MEDIUM-3: queryBead now accepts variadic extra args, queryBeadWithExtra eliminated
- LOW-3: resolveBDPath() called once in runRestoreRender, bdPath passed to all functions

Build + go vet clean. Pushed.

## Current Goal
RFC-003 Phase 1 implementation — all OC-assigned tasks complete, CX unblocked for 1.4 wire-up. Awaiting next assignment.

## Key Decisions
- party-brief-prompt.txt uses "the current agent" (not ${AGENT_ROLE}) so no shell expansion needed at call time — simpler, avoids envsubst dependency.
- Fallback output in prompt enforces 4-section invariant explicitly, preventing malformed briefs from reaching bead storage.
- commit_compact_offset() uses mktemp for collision safety under concurrent compactions; mv is atomic.
- restore.md Tier 0 query (Step 1.5) uses age < 2h threshold matching RFC-003 spec; stale brief shows warning but doesn't block recovery.

## Blockers

Waiting for OC approval to implement a 3-part infra fix: add cx-checkpoint-inject/tmux-inject to git, fix party-v2's worktree-relative admin-skills path, and delete worktree copies of daemon/admin-skills/.

5. Delete daemon/admin-skills/ from all 3 worktrees

title: cc checkpoint 2026-02-18 04:06
description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-18 04:06
**Role:** cc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1771405555-da4d0161

## Current Goal
Idle, awaiting OC. This session implemented idle-aware checkpoints, fixed checkpoint_content bead writing, and committed several patches from CX.

## Key Decisions
- Idle detection: JSONL mtime comparison, all-or-nothing, backstop interval 2h, grace period 2min
- Deadman threshold decoupled from health-check interval (fixed 10m)
- checkpoint_content handled in daemon main loop, writes beads via bd CLI directly
- Cycle key normalization: daemon replaces agent chk_id with cycle-EPOCH/60, preserves original as agent_chk_id label
- go install creates shadowing relay binary - always use go build -o instead

## Blockers
- (none)

## Next Steps
1. Await OC task assignment

## Session Commits (cc-wt branch)
- d18fa27 feat: idle detection (Chunk 1)
- bf24d41 fix: deadman threshold decoupling (Chunk 2)
- a8a8745 feat: wire idle detection into timer (Chunk 3)
- 7e3ac29 fix: guard idle detection bugs (CX review)
- b49b542 fix: grace period (external edit)
- 4fb1fa7 fix: checkpoint_content bead writing
- adec452 chore: .gitignore cleanup
- 715b6c1 feat: checkpoint cycle key normalization

description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-25 10:43 ET
**Role:** cc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772034178-6a36e457

## Current Goal
Investigated 3 structural daemon reliability bugs (orphan process leak, fatal fsnotify handling, missing crash forensics) per OC request. Sent detailed fix proposals to OC, awaiting go-ahead to implement.

## Key Decisions
- Bug 1 (orphan leak): Proposed pgrep sweep with /proc ownership verification — multi-session safe, catches processes invisible to PID-file cleanup
- Bug 2 (fatal fsnotify): Proposed log-and-continue with circuit breaker (10 rapid errors = fatal, resets every 30s) — keeps daemon alive through transient hiccups
- Bug 3 (crash forensics): Proposed startup banner, panic recovery with stack trace, signal logging, exit logging
- Bonus: Proposed admin-loop watchdog (kill -0 probe every 5min, restart if dead)

## Blockers
- Awaiting OC approval before implementing any changes

## Next Steps
1. Wait for OC go-ahead on which fixes to implement
2. Implement approved fixes in bin/party, watcher.go, main.go, admin-loop.sh
3. Commit changes on cc-wt branch

# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-25 12:08 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772039322-c7608461

## Current Goal
Discussing CX pane injection fix design with user. Explained pre-send wake-up (SIGWINCH via resize-pane), queue startup race (narrow, unlikely cause), and chunking need. User asking about wake-up frequency/impact.

## Key Decisions
- Pre-send wake-up is SIGWINCH (resize-pane -1/+1), not a keystroke — safe, no prompt interference
- Queue startup race is real but narrow (first few ms of daemon start), low priority vs wake-up and chunking
- Wake-up fires once per message injection, negligible cost (2 tmux cmds + 50ms)
- User has not yet approved implementation dispatch

## Blockers
- Awaiting user decision on whether to proceed with injection fixes

## Next Steps
1. Get user approval and dispatch CX for queue race + chunking + pre-send wake
2. Optionally dispatch CC for observability/gating improvements
3. Merge, rebuild, restart

title: oc checkpoint 2026-02-25 14:43
description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-25 14:43:38 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772048598-3681b8e5

## Current Goal
Finalize paste-buffer -p fix for large payload injection. Fix tested (20KB to both CX and CC), code reviewed by CX (no zombie code), ready to commit.

## Key Decisions
- paste-buffer needs -p flag to send bracketed paste boundaries so terminal apps recognize paste end and accept Enter as submit
- Without -p, Codex paste_burst.rs treats Enter as continuation (1024-byte chunks), not submit
- Threshold for Codex [Pasted Content] mode is 2048 bytes (inline below, compact above)
- All WakePane code removed cleanly — CX verified no zombie references, tests pass
- CC and CX both researched Codex internals: Rust/ratatui+crossterm, not Ink/React

## Blockers
- (none)

## Next Steps
1. Commit the -p fix to main
2. Restart session to pick up new binary
3. End-to-end test via relay send with 20KB payload
4. Then: process lifecycle fix (stop using disown for daemon/admin-loop)

---

Waiting for OC approval to implement a 3-part infra fix: add cx-checkpoint-inject/tmux-inject to git, fix party-v2's worktree-relative admin-skills path, and delete worktree copies of daemon/admin-skills/.

## Key Decisions
- Recommended canonical path `~/.party/admin-skills/` (consistent with existing `~/.party/` convention in party-v2)

3. Update install.sh to deploy admin-skills to ~/.party/admin-skills/

5. Delete daemon/admin-skills/ from all 3 worktrees

Phase C Task: W1 — Hard Cleanup (Checkpoint Removal)

## Goal
Remove all remaining checkpoint infrastructure. Atomic commit — everything goes together.

## Changes

### DELETE
- `daemon/internal/checkpoint/` — entire directory (content.go, 99 lines, zero imports)
- `daemon/scripts/admin/admin-checkpoint-cycle.sh` — dead code from Phase B

### EDIT daemon/cmd/relay/main.go
Remove the checkpoint_content deprecation stub (around lines 811-816):
```go
// Deprecated: checkpoint_content handler retained as no-op for in-flight producers. Remove in Phase C.
if env.To == "admin" && env.Kind == "checkpoint_content" {
    log.Printf("deprecated: checkpoint_content message from=%s, ignoring (RFC-006)", env.From)
    _ = logger.Log(logpkg.NewEvent(logpkg.EventTypeCheckpointAck, env.From, "admin").WithMsgID(env.MsgID).WithStatus("ignored:deprecated"))
    continue
}
```
Also remove the import of the checkpoint package if present.

### EDIT daemon/internal/log/events.go
Remove lines 73-74:
```go
EventTypeCheckpointRequest = "checkpoint_request"
EventTypeCheckpointAck     = "checkpoint_ack"
```
Update the comment on line 22 that lists these types.

### EDIT daemon/internal/log/events_test.go
The test uses EventTypeCheckpointAck — swap it for EventTypeMessageRouted or another live constant.

### EDIT daemon/cmd/context-capture/main.go
Remove Fallback B (legacy recovery/checkpoint bead queries, around lines 246-252):
```go
// Fallback B (transitional): legacy recovery or checkpoint beads
if id, body := queryBead(bdPath, "recovery", role, ""); id != "" {
    return id, body, "recovery"
}
if id, body := queryBead(bdPath, "checkpoint", role, ""); id != "" {
    return id, body, "checkpoint"
}
```

### EDIT bin/relay
Remove the `checkpoint)` case block (lines 68-136) and its usage line (line 140). This is the only producer of checkpoint_content messages.

### RENAME checkpoints.log → admin-health.log
- `daemon/scripts/admin/admin-health-check.sh` line 18
- `daemon/scripts/admin/admin-restart-cx.sh` line 17

### Verify
```
cd daemon && go build ./... && go test ./... && go vet ./...
grep -r "EventTypeCheckpoint" daemon/   # should return nothing
grep -r "internal/checkpoint" daemon/   # should return nothing
grep -r "checkpoints.log" daemon/       # should return nothing
```

### Commit
One atomic commit with all changes. Commit message: "fix: remove checkpoint infrastructure (RFC-006 Phase C)"

Relay me the result when done.

# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-20 ET
**Role:** cc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1771636063-282cd7af

## Current Goal
Waiting for OC approval to implement a 3-part infra fix: add cx-checkpoint-inject/tmux-inject to git, fix party-v2's worktree-relative admin-skills path, and delete worktree copies of daemon/admin-skills/.

## Key Decisions
- Recommended canonical path `~/.party/admin-skills/` (consistent with existing `~/.party/` convention in party-v2)
- Part 2 must come AFTER Part 3 is deployed (party-v2 exits with error if dir missing)
- Corrected OC's scope: all 3 worktrees (cc-wt, oc-wt, cx-wt) have daemon/admin-skills/, not just oc-wt/cx-wt

## Blockers
- Awaiting OC approval/acknowledgment before implementing anything

2. Add cx-checkpoint-inject + tmux-inject to daemon/scripts/ (copy from ~/.local/bin/)
3. Update install.sh to deploy admin-skills to ~/.party/admin-skills/
4. Fix party-v2 lines 64-69: replace SCRIPT_DIR-relative lookup with hardcoded path
5. Delete daemon/admin-skills/ from all 3 worktrees

description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-25 12:28 ET
**Role:** oc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1772040525-82df303e

## Current Goal
Testing pre-send wake-up fix with large payload (20KB RFC-004) to CX. Message was DROPPED — wake-up fired but prompt gating timed out. Gating heuristic is the remaining problem.

## Key Decisions
- Pre-send wake-up fix merged and deployed (a906775)
- Startup banner + crash forensics confirmed working (saw SIGTERM logged on restart)
- Large payload test: blocked→blocked→dropped. CX prompt visible but gating didn't detect it in time
- Prompt gating backoff (250ms→5s) is too aggressive for idle panes — this is the next fix needed

## Blockers
- Prompt gating heuristic fails after long idle despite wake-up fix

## Next Steps
1. Fix prompt gating for idle/Codex panes (reduce backoff, improve prompt regex, or allow ungated mode for CX)
2. Retry large payload test after fix
3. RFC-004 review still pending (was the test payload)

title: cc task 2026-02-26 19:00
description:
Phase B batch: RFC-006 cleanup tasks. Do all 4 in one commit on cc-wt:

B1: Remove checkpoint cycle from daemon/scripts/admin/admin-loop.sh
- Remove CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL var (line 18), LAST_CHECKPOINT (line 73), the checkpoint dispatch block (lines 80-83)
- Remove RELAY_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL from env doc comment (line 10)
- Update header comment (line 5) to reflect watchdog-only role
- Keep health check + daemon watchdog logic intact

B2: Delete session-end hook
- Delete ~/.claude/hooks/session-end.sh
- (Stop hook entry in settings.json already removed)

B4: Remove checkpoint skill
- Delete ~/.claude/commands/checkpoint.md (this is the /checkpoint skill definition)
- Also delete daemon/scripts/admin/admin-checkpoint-cycle.sh (the admin dispatch script)
- Also delete daemon/scripts/cx-checkpoint-inject if it exists

B5: Clean up party-stop checkpoint dispatch
- In bin/party-stop, remove Step 1b: pre-kill checkpoint dispatch (lines ~194-232)
- Keep Step 1 (async briefs) intact
- Also remove 'last-checkpoint-dispatch' and 'cx-chk-id' from the state cleanup list (line ~314)

Commit all together on cc-wt with a descriptive message.
notes:
[2026-02-26T19:02:09Z] cc -> oc
Phase B cleanup committed as 0908bdb on cc-wt. 4 files changed, -214 lines.

Done:
- B1: admin-loop.sh — removed checkpoint cycle (interval var, dispatch block, startup log). Header updated to watchdog-only.
- B4: Deleted admin-checkpoint-cycle.sh and cx-checkpoint-inject from repo.
- B5: party-stop — removed Step 1b (pre-kill /checkpoint dispatch + 15s wait). Removed last-checkpoint-dispatch and cx-chk-id from state cleanup list.

Not done (outside worktree per CLAUDE.md):
- B2: ~/.claude/hooks/session-end.sh — needs deletion by admin or install.sh
- B4 partial: ~/.claude/commands/checkpoint.md — same, outside worktree

Both external files need to be handled via install.sh or manual admin action. Want me to add deletion steps to install.sh instead?

[2026-02-26T19:02:29Z] oc -> cc
Cross-review CX's B3 (commit c61e41f on cx-wt). Changes in daemon/cmd/relay/main.go: checkpoint_content handler replaced with no-op deprecation stub. Verify: no remaining imports of internal/checkpoint, ACK event still logged correctly, no dead code paths. Severity-ordered findings.

[2026-02-26T19:03:18Z] cc -> oc
Cross-review of CX's B3 (commit c61e41f on cx-wt — checkpoint handler deprecation stub).

Verified:
- checkpoint import removed ✓ — no remaining references to internal/checkpoint in daemon/cmd/relay/
- internal/checkpoint/ package still exists on disk (correct — Phase C will hard-delete)
- ACK event logged correctly ✓ — uses EventTypeCheckpointAck with status 'ignored:deprecated'
- No dead code paths ✓ — handler still matches on env.To=="admin" && env.Kind=="checkpoint_content", logs deprecation, ACKs, continues

**No findings.** This is a clean, minimal change: -29 lines removed, +3 added. The no-op stub correctly:
1. Logs a deprecation warning via log.Printf (visible in relay.log)
2. Emits a structured checkpoint_ack event with status 'ignored:deprecated' (queryable in events.jsonl)
3. Continues past the handler (no injection, no bead write)

The only suggestion (not a finding): the log.Printf includes env.From which is fine for diagnostics. Could also include env.MsgID for correlation, but the logger.Log call already captures it.

Approved for merge.

[2026-02-26T19:03:34Z] oc -> cc
CX cross-review found additional cleanup needed. HIGH-1 is a false positive (B3 was CX's task on cx-wt, merges separately). But these are real:

HIGH-2: bin/party-stop async briefs (line ~164-171) use 'bd create --body' which doesn't exist. bd create uses '--description' not '--body'. Also uses '--type checkpoint' which should now be '--type task' or keep as-is for briefs. Fix the --body → --description flag.

MEDIUM-3: install.sh still references deleted scripts (cx-checkpoint-inject, admin-checkpoint-cycle.sh) at lines ~87, 128, 214. Remove these from install loops and drift checks.

MEDIUM-4: admin-health-check.sh (lines ~37-39, 54-58) uses last-checkpoint-dispatch timestamp for idle heuristic. With checkpoint cycle removed, this is incoherent. Remove or replace with a different activity signal.

MEDIUM-5: daemon/internal/adminpane/timer.go (lines ~18, 129, 134) still references checkpoint-cycle. Remove checkpoint-cycle from allowed timer commands and any test references.

Fix all on cc-wt and commit.

[2026-02-26T19:06:55Z] cc -> oc
CX cross-review findings fixed in commit 53d270b on cc-wt. 6 files, -128 lines.

- HIGH-2: party-stop bd create: --body → --description, --type checkpoint → --type task
- MEDIUM-3: install.sh: removed cx-checkpoint-inject and admin-checkpoint-cycle.sh from 3 locations (deploy loop, admin deploy loop, drift check)
- MEDIUM-4: admin-health-check.sh: replaced checkpoint-dispatch idle heuristic with JSONL mtime check (5min threshold). No more dependency on last-checkpoint-dispatch file.
- MEDIUM-5: timer.go: removed /checkpoint-cycle from allowedCommands, removed runCheckpointTicker goroutine + checkpointCycles field + CheckpointCycles() method. Start() now runs health ticker only. Updated timer_test.go (moved /checkpoint-cycle to rejected list) and recycler_integration_test.go (removed timer.checkpointCycles reference).

All tests pass (go test ./... clean). Pushed.

[2026-02-26T19:08:35Z] oc -> cc
Task B6: Rename admin-loop.sh → admin-watchdog.sh. All references must be updated atomically.

Files to change:
1. daemon/scripts/admin/admin-loop.sh → git mv to admin-watchdog.sh. Update internal refs:
   - pid file: admin-loop.pid → admin-watchdog.pid
   - heartbeat file: admin-loop.heartbeat → admin-watchdog.heartbeat  
   - log prefix: [admin-loop] → [admin-watchdog]
   - comment at top

2. daemon/scripts/admin/admin-health-check.sh — lines 62-71:
   - admin-loop.heartbeat → admin-watchdog.heartbeat
   - 'admin-loop' references in log messages

3. daemon/scripts/install.sh — lines 128, 214:
   - admin-loop.sh → admin-watchdog.sh in deploy loops and drift checks

4. bin/party — lines 72-76, 250, 276:
   - ADMIN_LOOP var name → ADMIN_WATCHDOG (or keep var name, just change paths)
   - admin-loop.sh → admin-watchdog.sh in path resolution
   - admin-loop.pid → admin-watchdog.pid

5. bin/party-stop — lines 246-271:
   - admin-loop references in kill step, pgrep pattern, pidfile cleanup
   - admin-loop.pid → admin-watchdog.pid
   - admin-loop.heartbeat → admin-watchdog.heartbeat

Use git mv for the rename. Commit on cc-wt.

[2026-02-26T19:10:49Z] cc -> oc
B6 complete — commit 767dfb9 on cc-wt. Atomic rename admin-loop.sh → admin-watchdog.sh across 5 files:

1. git mv daemon/scripts/admin/admin-loop.sh → admin-watchdog.sh (pid, heartbeat, log prefix, header)
2. admin-health-check.sh — heartbeat file + log refs
3. install.sh — deploy loop + drift check
4. bin/party — ADMIN_LOOP → ADMIN_WATCHDOG var, path, pid, pgrep, log file
5. bin/party-stop — kill step, pgrep pattern, cleanup list

Verified: zero 'admin-loop' references remain in bin/ or daemon/ (grep clean). Only operations.md has stale doc refs (not functional). Pushed.

title: cc checkpoint 2026-02-19 02:11
description:
# Checkpoint

**Generated:** 2026-02-19 02:12 ET
**Role:** cc
**Checkpoint ID:** chk-1771485070-5c260740

## Current Goal
RFC-003 Phase 1 complete — all chunks 1.1–1.6 applied to ~/.claude/hooks/pre-compact.sh, Phase 3 integration tests passed. Awaiting OC to initiate /5pass code review.

## Key Decisions
- Applied all patches manually (CX patch format is not standard unified diff; edited directly via Edit tool).
- Phase 1.5 captures CHECKPOINT_BEAD_ID from bd create stdout before Phase 1.4 worker is launched — correct ordering since vars are initialized to "" in 1.4 preamble then populated by 1.5 at runtime.
- Worker script written to /tmp with unique tmp_id, passes its own path as $7 so cleanup trap can self-delete.
- commit_compact_offset() called only after kill -0 confirms worker is alive (true two-phase: offset advances iff worker launched, not iff LLM call completes).
- dd used for transcript slicing (byte-precise); party-jsonl-filter handles format normalization.

## Blockers
- (none) — awaiting /5pass or OC next assignment.

## Next Steps
1. Respond to /5pass when OC initiates it
2. Address any issues found in 5-pass review
3. Phase 4 doc cleanup (RFC-002-STATUS.md, bd config session_brief type) — assigned to OC, not CC

Investigating why cc-party's relay daemon (PID 2347700) died silently at 23:21:39 on Feb 24. User explicitly asked for investigation only, no fixes.

- Identified orphaned admin-loop (PID 2122892 from 16:20) still running alongside dead admin-loop (PID 2347715)
- Daemon death is inconclusive — no OOM, no core dump, no manual kill, no journal entries

5. Send RFC-004 to CX for critique
